methods.description: Two-way hierarchical cluster analysis was used to classify sites and species into groups based on their similarities, revealing patterns in foraminiferal assemblages across different bays.

steps.description: All sites and the 35 common species were clustered into two groups by using two-way hierarchical cluster analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-way hierarchical cluster analysis on sites and species
outputs: clustered groups of sites and species

steps.description: A heat map of the relative abundance and distribution of individual species demonstrated that group I tended to be more abundant in Houshui Bay and that group II was richer in Yangpu Bay.
steps.searchable_summary: create heat map of species abundance and distribution

methods.description: CCA was applied to reveal the relationship of sites/species to environmental variables such as heavy metals, TOC, and PAHs, identifying the main factors influencing foraminiferal assemblages.

steps.description: CCA was applied to reveal the relationship of sites/species to heavy metals, TOC, and PAHs.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CCA to analyze relationship between sites/species and environmental variables

steps.description: Four axes were identified, and the first two together explained 41% of the variation in foraminiferal assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and interpret axes of variation from CCA

steps.description: Figure 10 shows the positioning of sites and species projected on the CCA biplots, where the arrows represent gradient directions of environmental variables.
steps.searchable_summary: create CCA biplots to visualize site and species positioning

methods.description: Collection of surface sediment samples from Houshui Bay and Yangpu Bay for laboratory analysis of foraminiferal and chemical properties.

steps.description: 30 surface sediments were collected from Houshui Bay and the outer part of Yangpu Bay.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediment samples from specified bays

steps.description: The upper 2 cm layer of undisturbed sediment was sampled for laboratory analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: sample upper 2 cm layer of sediment for analysis

steps.description: Approximately 100 cm3 of sediment was placed into plastic bottles containing 4% neutrally buffered formaldehyde solution and rose Bengal stain.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment samples with formaldehyde and rose Bengal stain
